summary: Implement FR-4.10 OpenSCAD animation with source-aware controls, sequential preview rendering, native and WASM parameter parity, owned cancellation, and bounded diagnostic history.

decisions.topic: Use a fixed source-aware animation frame domain
decisions.decision: Show animation controls only when executable $t use occurs in the active source or a reachable include/use dependency, and map a fixed 100-frame loop to $t values 0.00 through 0.99.
decisions.rationale: Lexical detection avoids false controls for comments and strings, dependency traversal respects project structure, and a fixed domain gives scrubbing and playback one deterministic frame identity across native and WASM paths.

decisions.topic: Backpressure animation through the ordinary preview render path
decisions.decision: Dispatch one preview frame at a time, wait for completion before scheduling the next target-FPS frame, pass $t as a transient request parameter, keep animation frames out of command history, and let Pause cancel only the animation-owned in-flight request.
decisions.rationale: Sequential dispatch prevents render-queue growth and supersession churn, transient parameters preserve the user's durable parameter state, and ownership-aware cancellation cannot terminate a newer manual render.

decisions.decision: Retain at most 1000 completed console runs while preserving every running job, track the cumulative number removed, and disclose that count visibly and in copied console history.
decisions.rationale: Animation can create many legitimate renders even when geometry caching is unavailable. Bounding completed metadata prevents unbounded long-session growth without silently presenting truncated history as complete.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-4.10 exposes accessible source-aware controls and renders exact, sequential, cancellable preview frames with native/WASM parity and bounded long-session state.
testEvidence.test: Start with missing animation modules and absent runtime $t transport, implement source/runtime/UI/native/WASM behavior under focused regressions, run a narrow real-engine browser walkthrough, execute the full browser unit and production-build gates, and complete three independent bounded reviews.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/animation/animation-source.test.ts tests/ui/animation/AnimationBar.test.tsx tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts --reporter=dot; three independent FR-4.10 adversarial reviews
red.outcome: RED: animation modules were initially absent and the runtime did not transport $t. Independent reviews then reproduced missing include/use dependency detection, unsafe pause ownership, silent frame-dispatch rejection, zero-based screen-reader frame identity, unbounded console-run metadata, and absent visible disclosure when old runs were removed. Each product defect received a focused assertion before correction.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run <8 affected files> --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd test -- --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd typecheck; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd build; cargo test --manifest-path src/native-engine/Cargo.toml parameters --locked; pnpm.cmd check:licenses all; pnpm.cmd check:provenance; git diff --check
green.outcome: GREEN: the affected denominator passed 8 files/84 tests and the complete browser unit denominator passed 188 files/1422 tests. The 800-by-800 in-app browser walkthrough showed the animation bar with real OpenSCAD 2026.06.12 preview geometry and no horizontal overflow. TypeScript, lint/source policy over 483 files, native parameter tests, production build, licenses, provenance, and diff hygiene exited 0. Runtime, UI/UX, and specification reviewers each returned literal 0 blocker / 0 critical / 0 major / 0 minor / 0 nit.

nearMisses.sought: Adversarial source detection, scheduling, cancellation, accessibility, runtime transport, and long-session inspection
nearMisses.confirmation: Three independent bounded reviews converted reachable-dependency, cancellation-ownership, visible-error, screen-reader identity, and console-retention edge cases into focused regressions before converging to strict zero.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
